title: Is Colorado good for van dwelling? - Request

transcript.text: For the request, for the request. This one's from Kyle Brown, and he goes,
Hey, I was thinking about moving to Colorado in my Grand Caravan due to layoffs here.
Is it easy to conceal yourself in Colorado, or is it similar to living in a big city now that weed is legal?
I'm actually trying to get a tattoo apprenticeship there.
So, is it easy to conceal yourself in Colorado?
Yeah, it's easy to conceal yourself anywhere.
I don't know what a Grand Caravan is, though.
If you have a big camper van, and you want to live out of a car,
You should sell your camper van and just buy a normal van, because you're going to get busted.
And you're going to have trouble parking, and it's not going to be fun, dude.
You don't need to live in something that big unless you've got a wife and kids, man.
But if you already have it, I guess you might as well try, but it's not going to be very easy.
Although, there's this Safeway in northeast Boulder, and there's always this big camper van that's parked there.
Like, days on end.
And I think there's people living out of it, and nobody's done anything.
Somebody told me, like, back in the, like, mid-90s, and they lived in Boulder for a while,
And they told me that it's actually legal to sleep in your car in Boulder.
I don't know if that's true or not, but, yeah, if you live in a van, you can pretty much live in your car anywhere.
You just move around, and I never really had a problem.
But I was smart.
You know, I'm not one of these brazen, stupid, nervy people who will just sleep in the same place all the time.
I'm good.
I'm stealthy.
I move around.
But I would have been scared shitless trying to do it out of a caravan, out of some big-ass camper van.
But, so let's see.
Is it easy to conceal yourself in Colorado, or is it similar to living in a big city now that weed is legal?
I don't understand the question.
Like, you're implying that it's more difficult, or is it similar to living in a big city?
It's, like, you're implying that it's harder to be anonymous in a big city than a small city.
It's easier to be anonymous in a big city, just like it's easier to hide a needle in a larger haystack than a smaller haystack.
The larger the city, the easier it is to be anonymous.
And now that weed's legal, so now that weed's legal, that's going to make it harder to be anonymous in a car.
Why is that?
Because all of the stoner, because all the people who live in van are stoners.
And so now all the van-dwelling stoners are going to move to Colorado.
I don't think that's true.
I had a lot of people who live in vans, live in vans because they're poor.
And a lot of poor people are not stoners.
It's the people who make money and the educated people who are the ones who smoke weed.
It's the poor people who don't.
You know, the poor people are the really, are the Nazis, you know.

The uneducated people who have, like, low-paying jobs and stuff, those are, like, the fundamentalist Christians who go to church every Sunday and think that everybody's going to burn in hellfire and think that weed should be illegal.
People live in, you know, so, so, but I mean, young people live in vans and young people smoke weed, so it is kind of a, you know, two-edged sword.

There are a lot of, like, you know, homeless people, like, or, like, bums begging for money in Boulder, and there might be, so I don't know if more, now that weed's legal, more people are going to come here to try to, but I mean, you can buy weed anywhere in the world, you know.
I mean, well, anywhere in the world, pretty much anywhere in America, it's pretty easy, so.
I think people, it will draw a lot of people, marijuana being legal to Colorado, but it'll be normal people, you know.
Not people live in vans.
Anyways, is it easy to conceal, so is it, how easy it is to conceal yourself in Colorado as opposed to other states?
I haven't lived in my van in Colorado, so I really don't know by, wait, hold on, I did.
I went to college in Fort Lewis and Durango, Colorado, and I lived in my van for two years in Colorado, and I lived in the same place.
I parked my van and just left it there all year, like, in the middle of this, like, this dirt road as it went down, so I had nothing around me, and nobody bugged me.

Once the cops, once I camped in the actual college campus and the cops busted me, even though it's not in their jurisdiction, the college campus should do it, and they made me go to court and I had to pay $40.

But once the cops busted me in town, and they were cool, they didn't give me a ticket or anything, and they just told me to camp outside the city limits, which is only, because it's a town, it was just outside, and that's legal.
You just passed the city limits, it was only a mile away.
You know, as long as you're outside the city limits, it's actually legal to sleep in your car.
The only thing you would want to worry about is vigilance, like, bandits and stuff.
But I don't see that happening in America, you know, unless you're in, like, a poor part, a crime-ridden place.
In which case, I don't know, depending on how hardcore you are, maybe you could just get a gun.
And if a bandit wants to rob your car, you could shoot him in the face and declare, uh, stand my ground or something.
I hate bandits, dude.
I fucking hate, I hate bike thieves, I hate, I hate crime.
In fact, I think I switched my, I switched my opinion on the George Zimmerman thing.

I did a video, what I think about Trayvon Martin and George Zimmerman, and I was siding with George Zimmerman, I mean Trayvon Martin, but now I'm going to side with George Zimmerman, because that kid, actually, no, I'm not.
I'm going to side with Trayvon Martin.
Because George Zimmerman had a gun, and he should have just pointed it at the kid, and then the kid would have run away.
Why do you let the kid jump you first?
Yeah, so I still side with Trayvon Martin.
Anyway, it has nothing to do with this video.
Sorry, I got way sidetracked.
But, um, I don't think you're going to get carjacked in Colorado.
You could camp anywhere.
In fact, I've done that.
Back in 96, I drove around, I lived in my van around Colorado, and Colorado's a great place.
Colorado's not, there's no crime.
In fact, Colorado, I think, is the best state in America.
People are very, like, the front range is very good, well-off economically.
There's not that many bad neighborhoods.
I think the only bad neighborhood, or the worst neighborhood, and I don't even know how bad it is, would be Commerce City in northeastern Denver.
But Boulder's, like, there's no crime at all.
Like, I know people who don't even lock their houses and stuff, you know.
Maybe I shouldn't have said that out loud, because I wouldn't want the criminals to come here.
But, um, but yeah, Colorado's a great place, man.
Oh my god, you cannot do, go wrong moving to Colorado.
Can't believe I'm saying that out loud.
I'm, like, inviting everybody to come to Colorado.
But, uh, because people have swarmed to Boulder over the last 20 years.
But it's been, it's, it's been, like, the right kind of people.
Like, athletic type of people.
Which I like.
Um, so yeah, do it, man.
Good luck.
Do it.
But, Grand Caravan?
I mean, you'd be living a little bit more luxurially than in a car, but you just made yourself a fucking target.
You just painted a huge target on yourself by having some big ol' van.
Or, I don't know what a Grand Caravan is.
A Grand Caravan is just, like, a big Dodge van or something like that.
And yeah, it's wicked, dude.
Have fun.
Hit it up.
You got a reliable American car.
An engine that's not going to blow up every fucking 2,000 miles.
So, have fun, Kyle.
So, have fun.
